mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent indicates labor market deterioration that suppresses wage growth and corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales and housing starts yields a statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month industrial production growth signals immediate supply-side capacity utilization that triggers short-term earnings revision cycles and positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 in the 5-day window following industrial production releases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 190.0 reflect entrenched inflation that forces central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are non-negative in over 55 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market expansion without slack, supporting earnings growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint modeling of payrolls and unemployment fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series baselines by more than 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly tracks aggregate corporate revenue expansion, establishing a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.02, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during periods of elevated policy rates signals supply-side stress that outweighs monetary support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in more than 65 percent of triggered observations.
